name: Competitive Landscape
description: Maps the competitive landscape for any product or company. Discovers competitors, builds feature comparison matrices, identifies positioning gaps, and surfaces underserved market opportunities.

productDescription.description: Description of the product or company to analyze (1-3 sentences)

productUrl.description: URL of the product website (optional — helps with feature discovery)

industry.description: Industry or vertical (optional — auto-detected from description)

maxCompetitors.description: Maximum number of competitors to analyze

fields.name: competitorMap
fields.description: Array of competitors with name, url, description, funding, marketPosition

fields.name: featureComparison
fields.description: Feature matrix with 5+ dimensions comparing all discovered competitors

fields.name: positioningGaps
fields.description: 2+ underserved positioning gaps with evidence from competitor analysis

fields.name: opportunityAreas
fields.description: Market opportunities with rough sizing and entry difficulty

invariants.invariant: PHASE 1: DISCOVERY. Before building the competitor map, create an artifact 'competitor-discovery.md' documenting your search strategy: queries used, sources checked, candidates found, and why each was included or excluded. This ensures the competitor list is comprehensive, not just the first 5 Google results.
invariants.measurement: Verify 'competitor-discovery.md' artifact exists before competitorMap is populated. Must show at least 3 different search queries.

do: Search 'alternatives to [product]', '[industry] market leaders 2026', 'top [category] tools comparison'
Check G2, Capterra, Product Hunt, and industry reports
dont: Use a single Google search and take the first 5 results
Skip the discovery artifact and go straight to the competitor map

invariants.invariant: Discover at least {{maxCompetitors}} competitors (default 5). For each competitor, provide: name, url, description (1-2 sentences), funding (if available), and marketPosition (leader/challenger/niche/emerging).
invariants.measurement: Count items in result.competitorMap. Must be >= maxCompetitors input value (default 5). Each entry must have all 5 fields.

do: { name: 'Competitor X', url: 'https://...', description: 'Enterprise-focused alternative with strong API', funding: 'Series B, $45M', marketPosition: 'challenger' }
dont: Include competitors without URLs or descriptions

invariants.invariant: Build a feature comparison matrix with at least 5 feature dimensions. Each dimension must be relevant to the product category and scored consistently across all competitors (e.g., yes/no/partial, or 1-5 scale). Include the target product in the comparison.
invariants.measurement: Count feature dimensions in result.featureComparison. Must be >= 5. All competitors + target product must be scored on every dimension.

do: Dimensions: 'API Access', 'Self-hosted Option', 'SSO/Enterprise Auth', 'Free Tier', 'Real-time Collaboration' — each competitor scored on all 5
dont: Score some competitors on different dimensions than others

invariants.invariant: Identify at least 2 underserved positioning gaps — market segments or feature combinations that no current competitor fully addresses. Each gap must include: gapDescription, evidence (what research showed this is underserved), targetAudience, and estimatedDemand (qualitative: high/medium/low with reasoning).
invariants.measurement: Count items in result.positioningGaps. Must be >= 2. Each must have gapDescription, evidence, targetAudience, and estimatedDemand.

do: Gap: 'No competitor offers self-hosted + real-time collaboration'. Evidence: '3 of 5 competitors are cloud-only; the 2 self-hosted options lack real-time features. Forum threads on Reddit (linked) show demand.'
dont: List gaps that existing competitors already fill well

invariants.invariant: For each opportunity area, provide: opportunityDescription, roughMarketSize (order-of-magnitude estimate with reasoning), entryDifficulty (easy/medium/hard with explanation), and timeToMarket (estimate).
invariants.measurement: Each item in result.opportunityAreas must have all 4 fields with substantive content (not just 'N/A').

do: { opportunityDescription: 'Vertical-specific solution for healthcare compliance', roughMarketSize: '$500M-1B (healthcare SaaS growing 15% YoY, compliance segment ~10% of that)', entryDifficulty: 'hard (HIPAA certification required)', timeToMarket: '12-18 months' }
dont: List opportunities without market size estimates
